notes: Run 46 (2026-05-17): 120-card window Koffing (sv09-091, pos 730) through Marill (sv08-073, pos 849). Found 6 fixes and 5 documented engine gaps (11 total). CONTINUATION_REQUIRED because target of 25 not reached. Next run starts at Marill | TEF | 64 | sv05-064 (position 851).

audit_ledger.db_effect_text: If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
audit_ledger.observed_behavior: Handler _bemusing_aroma applied StatusCondition.CONFUSED to player.active (self) on tails result instead of opp.active.
audit_ledger.expected_behavior: On tails, the opponent's Active Pokémon should become Confused.
audit_ledger.fix_description: Changed line 8176 of attacks.py: player.active → opp.active in the tails branch; updated event name from bemusing_aroma_self_confused to bemusing_aroma_opp_confused.

behavioral_evidence.description: Line 8176 was `player.active.status_conditions.add(StatusCondition.CONFUSED)` where player is the attacker, not the opponent.

audit_ledger.db_effect_text: Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
audit_ledger.observed_behavior: Handler _fade_out called comfey.energy_attached.clear() discarding attached energy source cards to nowhere, then moved Pokémon to hand. Energy cards were lost.
audit_ledger.expected_behavior: Energy source cards tracked via att.source_card_id should be found in player.discard or player.deck, moved to Zone.HAND, and appended to player.hand before clearing attachments.
audit_ledger.fix_description: Rewrote _fade_out to follow the _tuck_tail pattern: iterate energy_attached, find source card via att.source_card_id in player.discard then player.deck, move to player.hand. Then clear attachments and bounce Pokémon.

behavioral_evidence.description: Lines 12565-12566 cleared energy_attached and tools_attached without returning source cards. Reference: _tuck_tail at line ~2047 shows the correct bounce-to-hand energy return pattern.

audit_ledger.db_effect_text: Once during your turn, you may discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, put 5 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon.
audit_ledger.observed_behavior: Registered as register_passive_ability only — no USE_ABILITY button shown, no handler fired. sv08-059 Magneton has the correct handler (_overvolt_discharge) but svp-153 did not.
audit_ledger.expected_behavior: Should use the same _overvolt_discharge handler and _cond_overvolt_discharge condition as sv08-059.
audit_ledger.fix_description: Changed abilities.py: replaced register_passive_ability('svp-153', 'Overvolt Discharge') with register_ability('svp-153', 'Overvolt Discharge', _overvolt_discharge, condition=_cond_overvolt_discharge).

behavioral_evidence.description: Line 5551 was register_passive_ability. sv08-059 at lines 5322-5323 uses the full register_ability call with handler and condition. svp-153 is an alternate print of the same card.

audit_ledger.db_effect_text: Once during your turn, you may discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, put 5 damage counters on your opponent's Active Pokémon.
audit_ledger.observed_behavior: Registered as register_passive_ability only. Comment incorrectly labeled this card as 'Magnezone' — it is Magneton (alternate print of sv08-059).
audit_ledger.expected_behavior: Should use the same _overvolt_discharge handler and _cond_overvolt_discharge condition as sv08-059.
audit_ledger.fix_description: Changed abilities.py: replaced register_passive_ability('svp-159', 'Overvolt Discharge') with register_ability('svp-159', 'Overvolt Discharge', _overvolt_discharge, condition=_cond_overvolt_discharge). Also corrected misleading 'Magnezone' comment to 'Magneton'.

behavioral_evidence.description: Line 5553 was register_passive_ability with incorrect 'Magnezone' comment. svp-159 is an alternate Magneton print sharing the same ability as sv08-059.

audit_ledger.db_effect_text: If 1 of your other Ancient Pokémon used an attack during your last turn, this attack does 150 more damage.
audit_ledger.observed_behavior: Handler _koraidon_onslaught_flag checked any(p.last_attack_name for p in player.bench) — no Ancient type filter. Non-Ancient benched Pokémon could incorrectly trigger the bonus.
audit_ledger.expected_behavior: The bonus should only apply if a benched Ancient Pokémon (as identified by _is_ancient()) has last_attack_name set.
audit_ledger.fix_description: Added _is_ancient(p) filter to the any() check in _koraidon_onslaught_flag: any(p.last_attack_name for p in player.bench if _is_ancient(p)).

behavioral_evidence.description: Line 16079 had `any(p.last_attack_name for p in player.bench)`. The _is_ancient() function at line 121 checks the Ancient subtype. DB text explicitly says 'Ancient Pokémon'.

audit_ledger.db_effect_text: Once during your turn, you may heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
audit_ledger.observed_behavior: Handler _confectionary_gift used `caster = player.active` instead of `_find_in_play(player, action.card_instance_id)`. When Alcremie ex is on the bench, the Active Pokémon's ability_used_this_turn flag was incorrectly set instead of Alcremie ex's flag.
audit_ledger.expected_behavior: Caster should be identified by action.card_instance_id, allowing the ability to work correctly whether Alcremie ex is Active or Benched.
audit_ledger.fix_description: Changed `caster = player.active` to `caster = _find_in_play(player, action.card_instance_id)` in _confectionary_gift, matching the pattern used by _mammoth_hauler and other ability handlers.

behavioral_evidence.description: Line 3252 was `caster = player.active`. actions.py line 712 generates USE_ABILITY actions for all _in_play() Pokémon including bench, so bench Alcremie ex can trigger this ability. _mammoth_hauler (same file, line 3289) correctly uses _find_in_play().

audit_ledger.db_effect_text: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent's Pokémon (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), draw 2 cards.
audit_ledger.observed_behavior: Registered as _noop in trainers.py. No triggered draw-on-damage effect implemented.
audit_ledger.expected_behavior: When the holder is hit by an opponent's attack while Active, the holder's player draws 2 cards (triggered after damage resolution).
audit_ledger.fix_description: ENGINE GAP: Implementing a draw-on-damage triggered effect requires hooking into the damage resolution pipeline in base.py/_apply_damage to check attached tool cards. This is a structural change that cannot be safely isolated in this PR.

behavioral_evidence.description: trainers.py registers _noop for sv06-158. The effect requires post-damage callback hooks that do not exist in the current engine architecture.

audit_ledger.db_effect_text: Attacks used by the Pikachu ex this card is attached to do 50 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
audit_ledger.observed_behavior: Registered as _noop in trainers.py with comment '+50 for Pikachu ex'. No damage modifier hookup.
audit_ledger.expected_behavior: When Pikachu ex has Light Ball attached, its attacks should deal +50 damage to opponent Pokémon ex. Requires a tool-based damage modifier in the _apply_damage pipeline.
audit_ledger.fix_description: ENGINE GAP: Tool-attached damage modifiers against specific targets require reading attached_tools during damage calculation. Not implemented in current architecture.

behavioral_evidence.description: trainers.py _noop registration for me02.5-191. The effect requires per-attack damage modifier hookup similar to how type-based boosts are handled, but keyed on the attached tool card.

audit_ledger.db_effect_text: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, that player takes 1 fewer Prize card. This effect of your Legacy Energy can't be applied more than once per game.
audit_ledger.observed_behavior: Handler _legacy_energy in energies.py only sets provides = [EnergyType.ANY]. The KO prize-reduction effect is not implemented.
audit_ledger.expected_behavior: On KO of Legacy Energy holder (by attack damage), opponent takes 1 fewer prize. One-time effect per game.
audit_ledger.fix_description: ENGINE GAP: Prize reduction on KO requires a hook in the check_ko / prize-taking path (base.py) to check for attached Legacy Energy. The one-per-game constraint also requires global state tracking. Structural change deferred.

behavioral_evidence.description: energies.py _legacy_energy at line ~199 only sets EnergyType.ANY provision. No prize callback logic present. check_ko in base.py does not inspect attached energy cards for prize modifiers.

audit_ledger.db_effect_text: If the Lillie's Pokémon this card is attached to is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent's Pokémon, your opponent takes 1 fewer Prize card.
audit_ledger.observed_behavior: Registered as _noop in trainers.py. No prize-reduction-on-KO effect implemented.
audit_ledger.expected_behavior: When a Lillie's Pokémon with Lillie's Pearl is KO'd by attack damage, the opponent takes 1 fewer prize card.
audit_ledger.fix_description: ENGINE GAP: Same architectural limitation as Legacy Energy. check_ko in base.py does not inspect attached tool cards for prize reduction callbacks.

behavioral_evidence.description: trainers.py _noop registration for sv09-151. Prize-on-KO hook not present in base.py check_ko path.

audit_ledger.db_effect_text: Choose 1 of your Benched N's Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.
audit_ledger.observed_behavior: Handler _night_joker auto-selects the highest-damage non-copy attack from bench N's Pokémon (iterates all bench, tracks best_damage). Does not ask the player to choose which Pokémon or attack.
audit_ledger.expected_behavior: Player should be presented with a choice of available attacks from benched N's Pokémon and select one to use.
audit_ledger.fix_description: ENGINE GAP: Auto-selection is an AI-optimal approximation. Implementing player choice requires a multi-step ChoiceRequest (choose Pokémon, then choose attack index). Deferring to preserve current AI simulation behavior.

behavioral_evidence.description: attacks.py lines 2524-2551 show the handler iterating bench N's Pokémon and tracking best_damage. No ChoiceRequest is issued to the player for Pokémon/attack selection.
